_comment: PROJECT DNA — read this FIRST every session (Continuity + Adaptation protocols). A fresh chat reads this file to understand WHAT the project is, not just how far along it is. Populate it during initialization by detecting the stack from the repo; keep it current as decisions are made.
one_liner: Enterprise multi-agent AI development team framework with cross-session brain memory, usable across IDEs.
description: A framework that turns any AI coding assistant into a 17-agent engineering team. Agents are markdown skill files; durable state lives in JSON/TOML brain files; auto-activation files wire it into Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Antigravity, Trae, Codex CLI, and VS Code.
north_star: Make AI assistants behave like a complete, project-aware engineering team — continuous across sessions and consistent across IDEs.

conventions.folder_structure: .claude/skills (source of truth) mirrored to .cursor/skills and .windsurf/skills; brain templates in src/ai-team/brain; live brain in .ai-team/brain
conventions.code_style: max 1000 lines per file (warn at 700); skill files <500 line body ideal

glossary.definition: Durable cross-session memory: JSON/TOML files under .ai-team/brain/
A markdown file defining an agent persona, standards, and quality gates
The 4 cross-cutting behaviors in _core-protocols.md: Continuity, Adaptation, Self-Evolution, Clarification
Security agent can block deployment on any Critical/High finding
SRE agent can hold deployment until SLOs + error budgets are defined

key_decisions.rationale: Skill files need room for templates/examples

key_decisions.decision: Add 4 agents: ux-designer, data-engineer, sre, mobile-engineer
Add 4 Core Protocols + Project DNA + propose-don't-edit self-evolution
key_decisions.rationale: Make skills project-aware, continuous, and safely self-improving

constraints: skill mirrors (.claude/.cursor/.windsurf) must stay in sync
non_goals: Not a runtime/app — it does not execute application code
Not tied to a single IDE
